meta.game: Golden State Warriors at LA Clippers

GS.headline: Checkmates and closeouts: Inside the NBA's quiet chess boom
GS.description: NBA players are spending more time than ever working on the boards -- but we're not talking rebounds.

GS.headline: Golden State takes on Los Angeles for conference matchup
GS.description: Golden State Warriors (37-44, 10th in the Western Conference) vs. Los Angeles Clippers (41-40, ninth in the Western Conference)

GS.headline: Golden State Warriors vs. Sacramento Kings: Game Highlights
GS.description: Golden State Warriors vs. Sacramento Kings: Game Highlights

GS.headline: Devin Carter scores 29 points to lead the Kings to a 124-118 win over the Warriors
GS.description: — Devin Carter scored a career-high 29 points and the Sacramento Kings missed an opportunity to improve their draft position by beating the Golden State Warriors 124-118 on Friday night.

GS.headline: NBA's Friday slate sees at least 168 out with injury or illness
GS.description: Around the NBA, no fewer than 168 players were held out of Friday's games, including reigning MVP Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, three-time MVP Nikola Jokic and 14 members of the Grizzlies.

LAC.headline: Golden State takes on Los Angeles for conference matchup
LAC.description: Golden State Warriors (37-44, 10th in the Western Conference) vs. Los Angeles Clippers (41-40, ninth in the Western Conference)

LAC.headline: Avdija erupts for 35 as Trail Blazers pull away from Clippers
LAC.description: — Deni Avdija scored 35 points, Donovan Clingan had 18 points and 13 rebounds, and the Portland Trail Blazers beat the Los Angeles Clippers 116-97 on Friday night to take the inside track for the eighth seed in the Western Conference.

LAC.headline: LA Clippers vs. Portland Trail Blazers: Game Highlights
LAC.description: LA Clippers vs. Portland Trail Blazers: Game Highlights

LAC.headline: Los Angeles faces Portland, seeks 5th straight road win
LAC.description: Los Angeles Clippers (41-39, eighth in the Western Conference) vs. Portland Trail Blazers (40-40, ninth in the Western Conference)

LAC.headline: Thunder claim No. 1 overall seed in NBA with win
